summary: Frontend re-test (iteration 2). All testIDs from review_request are now present and functional. Flows verified end-to-end: admin login → dashboard (Juan Pérez / Cuenta administrador / dashboard-sales-today / sync-status-bar / 4 tiles / logout-button) → Venta (6 product cards, search, scan-button, tap product → cart-fab) → Scanner modal (manual barcode 7501055309627 adds Coca Cola) → Cart modal (qty-plus/minus) → checkout-button → payment-modal (efectivo + cash-received-input) → ticket-modal (print-button + new-sale-button). Catálogos: 4 sub-tabs (tab-products/companies/stores/users), 6 product rows, add-button opens form with save-button and wholesale-switch (toggle works). Gastos: expense-concept/amount/notes/save creates row visible in list. Reportes: range-today/week/month/all chips all switch filter, report-sales-total renders. Logout clears session and returns to login. Cajero login (cajero@pos.com): name='María López', role='Cuenta cajero', tile-catalogos hidden, bottom tab Catálogos hidden (4 tabs only).

minor.issue: After Mongo pos_database was dropped in this iteration setup, seed_data() did not auto-rerun until backend was restarted (FastAPI startup hook ran on first boot only). Restarted backend manually to re-seed. Recommend a healthcheck or idempotent seed on every startup.

ui_bugs.issue: Bottom-tab tabBarTestID prop on Tabs.Screen is NOT propagated to web DOM. <a role='tab'> elements have no data-testid attribute, so [data-testid='tab-venta' | tab-dashboard | tab-catalogos | tab-gastos | tab-reportes] selectors do not match in web preview. Tabs are functional via text/role. Works on native; web needs a workaround (e.g., wrap each Tabs.Screen with a custom tabBarButton that passes testID).

action_items: Make seed_data() idempotent or run on every startup so dropping the database is automatically recovered (currently it ran but only because backend was restarted; in production a deploy without restart would leave DB empty after drop).

Workaround tabBarTestID web limitation: provide a custom tabBarButton in Tabs.Screen options that renders a Pressable with testID, or use tabBarAccessibilityLabel + role-based selectors in tests as the agreed contract.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') is deprecated in FastAPI; migrate to lifespan context.
Low-stock threshold and other thresholds appear hardcoded in server.py; consider env vars.

Frontend uses programmatic .click() to bypass occasional 'Element is outside of the viewport' on web; this is a Playwright/RNW interaction quirk, not a product bug, but tests should standardize on JS-click for RNW pressables in modals.

success_rate.backend: 100% (auth login admin+cajero verified live; full pytest 17/17 from iteration_1 still applicable)
success_rate.frontend: ~95% (every testID in the spec was located and exercised; only the bottom-tab tabBarTestID does not render to web DOM)

seed_data_creation: Did not create new seed; backend restart re-ran seed_data() to repopulate Juan Pérez admin, María López cajero, 1 empresa, 1 tienda, 6 productos. Test created TEST_renta expense and 1-2 TEST sales via cash payment.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All flows green. Only outstanding item is tabBarTestID not surfacing as data-testid on web; tests should fall back to page.get_by_role('tab', name='Venta'|'Catálogos'|...) for bottom-tab navigation. Use JS .click() (page.evaluate) for buttons inside RN modals when 'Element is outside of the viewport' errors appear with native Playwright clicks.
rca_of_issue: Initial admin login returned 401 because pos_database collections were dropped in setup but FastAPI startup seeding had already run on the previous boot (count_documents>0 short-circuit could not save us since the collections were now empty AND running process was not restarted). Restarting backend triggered seed_data() which detected 0 companies and re-inserted users+products. Mitigation: convert seed_data into an idempotent upsert and run it on a periodic background task or an admin endpoint, OR have it run inside lifespan startup so any reload re-checks.
